title: Rights-card / explainer content JSON Schema + single-source render to site + dataset
context: Know-Your-Rights content must be authored once and rendered to both the static site and the machine-readable dataset so the human-facing page and the partner-reusable record can never drift. This task defines the JSON Schema (Ajv-validated) for the rights-card / explainer format and wires it into a single-source build that produces both the site and the dataset export. The schema is the contract that all content tasks and the content-lint suite (task 004) build against. Depends on the monorepo skeleton (task 002).
objective: Define and implement the Ajv-validated JSON Schema for the rights-card / explainer content format, with all required fields, and wire it into a single-source build pipeline that renders content to both the static site and the machine-readable CC-BY dataset export. Schema validation must run in CI; a schema-invalid content file must fail the build.
acceptanceCriteria: JSON Schema defined in packages/kyr-schema with required fields: jurisdiction, scope-level (general vs jurisdiction-specific), topic/scenario, plain-language right (as general legal information, not advice), citations[], 'what you can do / who to contact', not-legal-advice banner, readingLevel, lastVerified, validUntil, reviewer sign-off record, license, and provenance
Schema validated with Ajv in strict mode; schema-invalid content files fail the CI build with a clear error
A single-source build renders content records to both the static site (HTML pages) and a machine-readable dataset (JSON/YAML export) from the same source files, with no manual sync step
At least one fixture content record passes schema validation and renders correctly to both outputs
The not-legal-advice banner and jurisdiction label fields are required (non-nullable) in the schema, enforcing the policy from task 001
pnpm build && pnpm test pass with the schema wired in; schema validation runs as part of CI
Output dataset is licensed CC-BY-4.0; license field is required in the schema
output: A merged PR containing the rights-card / explainer JSON Schema (packages/kyr-schema), Ajv validation wired into CI, and a single-source build pipeline that renders content to both the static site and the CC-BY machine-readable dataset export.
